title: BiCut: A Solver-Agnostic Bilevel Optimization Compiler with Correctness Certificates and Bilevel Intersection Cuts

venue_targets.reach: Mathematical Programming Series A (only if facet-defining proof succeeds)

definitions.name: Bilevel Linear Program (BLP)
definitions.formal: min_{x in X} {d^T x + e^T y : C x + D y <= h, y in S(x)} where S(x) = argmin_{y >= 0} {c^T y : A y <= b + B x}
definitions.notes: Optimistic formulation. Leader variables x in R^{n_x}, follower variables y in R^{n_y}. Lower-level data: A in R^{m x n_y}, b in R^m, B in R^{m x n_x}, c in R^{n_y}. Upper-level coupling: C in R^{p x n_x}, D in R^{p x n_y}, h in R^p.

definitions.name: Mixed-Integer Bilevel Linear Program (MIBLP)
definitions.formal: BLP with integrality: x_I in Z for index set I subset {1..n_x}, y_J in Z for index set J subset {1..n_y}
definitions.notes: KKT conditions inapplicable when |J| > 0 (non-convex lower level). VF reformulation always correct but NP-hard to solve for MILP lower levels.

definitions.name: Bilevel Feasible Set
definitions.formal: S_BF = {(x,y) : C x + D y <= h, A y <= b + B x, y >= 0, c^T y <= phi(x)}
definitions.notes: Non-convex in general. The set of jointly feasible (x,y) with y optimal for lower level. Closed set (non-strict inequality).

definitions.name: Bilevel-Infeasible Set
definitions.formal: B_bar = {(x,y) : C x + D y <= h, A y <= b + B x, y >= 0, c^T y > phi(x)}
definitions.notes: Open set (strict inequality). Finite union of relatively open polyhedra for LP lower level (Proposition P1). Boundary points lie in S_BF, resolving open-set ray-exit issue.

definitions.name: Lower-Level Value Function
definitions.formal: phi(x) = min{c^T y : A y <= b + B x, y >= 0}

properties.lp_lower: Piecewise linear, continuous, convex on domain X_ell

definitions.name: Critical Region
definitions.formal: CR_B = {x in X_ell : basis B is optimal for lower-level LP at x}
properties: Each CR_B is a convex polyhedron in x-space
Critical regions partition X_ell (up to shared boundaries)
phi(x) = c^T A_B^{-1}(b_B + B_B x) is affine on each CR_B
Finitely many distinct bases: at most C(m, n_y)

components.var_types: (|I|, |J|) counting integer variables in x and y

definitions.name: Correctness Certificate
definitions.formal: Cert(P, R) = conjunction of verified preconditions ensuring R is exact for P

theorems.name: Polyhedrality of the Bilevel-Infeasible Set
theorems.statement: For a BLP with LP lower level where the lower level is feasible and bounded for all x in X_ell, B_bar is a finite union of relatively open polyhedra indexed by the critical regions of the lower-level parametric LP.
theorems.novelty: Partially folklore. Parametric LP decomposition is Gal 1995. Identifying B_bar as Balas forbidden set is the new framing.
theorems.proof_sketch: The lower-level LP has finitely many optimal bases {B_1,...,B_K}. Each defines CR_{B_k}. On CR_{B_k}, phi(x) = c^T A_{B_k}^{-1}(b_{B_k} + B_{B_k} x) is affine. B_bar restricted to CR_{B_k} x R^{n_y} is the intersection of polyhedra (joint feasibility, lower-level feasibility) with an open halfspace (c^T y > phi_{B_k}(x)). Union over K bases gives B_bar as finite union of relatively open polyhedra.
theorems.open_set_resolution: B_bar is open (strict inequality). For intersection cuts: boundary = {(x,y) : c^T y = phi(x)} consists of bilevel-feasible points. Ray from interior of B_bar reaches boundary continuously, so inf{t > 0 : exits B_bar} = min{t > 0 : reaches boundary}. No epsilon-perturbation needed.

assumptions.text: Lower level feasible and bounded for all x in X_ell

theorems.name: Bilevel Intersection Cut Separation
theorems.statement: Given an LP relaxation vertex (x_hat, y_hat) in B_bar, a valid bilevel intersection cut separating it from S_BF can be computed via: (1) critical region identification, (2) ray-boundary intersection along simplex directions, (3) Balas formula. The resulting cut is valid for conv(S_BF).

complexity.expected: O(N * LP(n_y, m)) under Conjecture C1 (O(1) CR transitions per ray)
complexity.note: N = number of non-basic variables. Worst case exponential in n_y.

theorems.novelty: New: applying Balas framework to bilevel-infeasible sets with critical-region ray-tracing.

conjecture_c1.statement: For BLPs with bounded coefficients, expected CR transitions per ray is O(1) under smoothed analysis
conjecture_c1.impact_if_false: Oracle impractical for n_y > 15. Paper reports results on tractable subset only.

theorems.name: Finite Convergence of the Bilevel Cut Loop
theorems.statement: Under non-degeneracy of the LP relaxation, the iterative bilevel intersection cut procedure terminates in finitely many iterations.
theorems.proof: Step 1: Each bilevel intersection cut is a linear inequality valid for S_BF, hence valid for conv(S_BF). Step 2: LP relaxation polyhedron P_LP contains conv(S_BF). Step 3: Each cut strictly separates current LP vertex from conv(S_BF) (vertex is in B_bar, hence not in conv(S_BF)). Under non-degeneracy, the cut removes this vertex. Step 4: P_LP has finitely many vertices. Each iteration removes at least one. Step 5: Terminate in at most |vertices(P_LP)| iterations. Step 6: Cut-induced degeneracy handled by Bland's rule or lexicographic perturbation.
theorems.worst_case_bound: C(m+p+R, n_x+n_y) iterations where R = cuts added. Exponential in dimension.
theorems.novelty: Adaptation to non-convex forbidden set via conv(S_BF) argument.

assumptions.text: Oracle finds cut when point is in B_bar

theorems.name: Facet-Defining Conditions (STRETCH GOAL)
theorems.statement: For a BLP with LP lower level, conditions under which a bilevel intersection cut defines a facet of conv(S_BF) relative to aff(S_BF).

dimensional_analysis.unique_response: If y*(x) unique for all x, then S_BF is n_x-dimensional in R^{n_x+n_y}. Facets are (n_x-1)-dimensional.
dimensional_analysis.degenerate_response: If lower-level optimal face has dimension k >= 1, S_BF has dimension n_x + k.
dimensional_analysis.relative_facets: All facet statements are relative to aff(S_BF), not the ambient space.

theorems.known_obstruction: Original (A2) unique-response + (A3) full-dimensionality assumptions are contradictory when n_y >= 2. Fixed by working relative to aff(S_BF).
theorems.fallback: If proof fails: computational evidence of cut strength. Paper viable at JOC without this.

theorems.name: Compiler Soundness
theorems.statement: If Typecheck(P) = sigma and Select(sigma) returns R in rho_exact(sigma), then opt(R(P)) = opt(P).
theorems.proof: Case analysis on sigma. LP lower level: STRONG_DUALITY exact by LP duality. Convex+CQ: KKT exact by Dempe 2002. Mixed-integer bounded: VF exact by Outrata 1990. General: VF always mathematically correct (not always efficiently computable).
theorems.vf_caveat: VF reformulation is always correct but NP-hard to solve for MILP lower levels. 'Always valid' means mathematical correctness, not computational efficiency.

theorems.name: Cut Validity Under Zero-Coefficient Lifting
theorems.statement: A valid inequality alpha^T(x,y) >= beta for S_BF lifts to the reformulated MILP space with zero auxiliary coefficients.
theorems.proof: Any (x,y,lambda,z) in F_R projects to (x,y) in S_BF by P3. So alpha^T(x,y) >= beta holds.
theorems.strength_caveat: Zero-coefficient lifting ignores auxiliary variable structure. Weakest possible lifting. Non-trivial lifting exploiting complementarity is future work.

theorems.name: Selection Soundness
theorems.statement: rho(sigma) only selects reformulations whose preconditions are implied by sigma.
theorems.proof: Finite case analysis on selection table (4 reformulation types, 5 signature classes).

proof_architecture.bridge: L1 connects cut theory (P1,P2,T1) to compiler theory (P3). Cuts derived in (x,y)-space, lifted to MILP space.

new: P1: B_bar as Balas forbidden set

separation_oracle.name: BILEVEL_SEPARATE
separation_oracle.output: Valid bilevel intersection cut or NO_CUT
steps: Check bilevel feasibility: solve lower-level LP at x_hat
Identify critical region from optimal basis (cache lookup)
For each simplex ray: compute exit point from B_bar via critical-region ray-tracing
Express in original variables, lift to MILP space (zero aux coefficients)

find_exit_point.method: Within each CR, phi is affine. Solve linear equation for ray-boundary intersection. If outside CR, find CR exit, move to next CR, repeat.
find_exit_point.warm_starting: Reuse LP basis from previous CR

degeneracy.fallback: Enumerate adjacent bases (at most n_y per degenerate vertex)

numerical_stability.coefficient_scaling: Scale cut to have max coefficient = 1
numerical_stability.solver_tolerance: Add 1e-7 slack to cut RHS for numerical safety

complexity.expected: O(N * LP(n_y, m)) under Conjecture C1
complexity.practical_limit: n_y <= 200 if C1 holds; n_y <= 15 worst case

lp_exact.method: Solve parametric LP with basis tracking and warm-starting
lp_exact.subgradient: nabla_x phi(x) = lambda^T B where lambda is dual optimal

milp_approximate.method: LP relaxation lower bound + MILP sampling + interpolation
milp_approximate.error_bound: L * min_k ||x - x_k|| where L is estimated Lipschitz constant
milp_approximate.honest_statement: Correct but NP-hard. Mathematical safety net, not practical for large MILP lower levels.

reformulation_selection.cost_model: Linear regression on log(solve_time) calibrated on BOBILib. Fallback: heuristic multipliers.

main_pipeline.compilation_time: < 10 seconds for typical instances (excluding cuts and solving)

complexity_analysis.bottleneck: Separation oracle for large n_y. Practical performance depends on Conjecture C1.
complexity_analysis.laptop_feasibility: All algorithms run on single CPU core. No GPU. < 16GB RAM. Full evaluation: ~4000 CPU-hours.

phase_0.goal: Prototype gate: separation oracle on 50 instances

phase_1.goal: Core compiler + cut engine on SCIP

phase_4.goal: Stretch goals (facet proof, VF lifting, CPLEX)

primary_claims.claim: >=10% geometric mean root gap closure on LP-lower instances with cuts
Auto-selection beats default KKT on >=5 instances with >=2x speedup
Certificates catch >=10% of unsound KKT applications

evaluation_plan.primary_comparison: BiCut vs BilevelJuMP, both on Gurobi (same solver, isolates reformulation quality)
evaluation_plan.secondary_comparison: BiCut vs MibS (practical baseline, acknowledges solver difference)

red_team_mitigations.fix: Formal argument: boundary in S_BF, inf=min
Downgraded to Lemma with strength caveat
epsilon-perturbation + bounded error + basis enumeration fallback

honest_assessment.math_grade: B (with upside to A- if T2 facet-defining succeeds)
honest_assessment.key_risk: Conjecture C1 (separation oracle practical efficiency). If false, cut contribution limited to small instances.
